features.summary: Bank of America reported Q2 2022 net income of $6.2 billion ($0.73 per share) with strong operating leverage, growing revenue 6% while expenses rose only 1.5%. Net interest income increased 21% year-over-year to $12.4 billion, driven by higher rates and loan growth. The company achieved its fourth consecutive quarter of operating leverage with loans growing to over $1 trillion (12% year-over-year) and maintained strong asset quality with charge-offs still 50% below pre-pandemic levels.
features.management_outlook: Management provided strong NII guidance of at least $900 million to $1 billion increase in Q3, with further acceleration expected in Q4, with the vast majority flowing to the bottom line. They expect mid-to-high single digit loan growth for the full year and modest deposit growth going forward. The company plans to build capital toward the new 10.9% CET1 requirement by January 2024 while continuing to return capital to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.
features.risks: Key risks include potential economic recession concerns, though management notes strong customer resilience with record spending levels and elevated deposit balances. The company faces regulatory expense pressures ($425 million in Q2) and leveraged finance markdowns ($300 million). Rising interest rates could impact mortgage pipeline and securities-based lending demand, while deposit competition may increase as rates rise further.
features.key_drivers: Primary earnings drivers include significant NII expansion from rising rates and strong loan growth across all segments, particularly commercial loans up 15% year-over-year. The company benefits from a high-quality, diversified loan portfolio with 92% of commercial loans either investment-grade or secured. Digital engagement continues growing with 20% increase in digital sales, supporting expense discipline and operating leverage.
